lesson: Use a list as a stack, pushing openers and popping+matching against a bracket-pair dict, to check balance in one O(n) pass.
Convert a string to a list and repeatedly call .pop() to reverse it via a stack instead of slicing.
Build a FIFO queue from two stacks: push onto in_stack always, only flip into out_stack when it's empty, giving O(1) amortized dequeue.
Use collections.OrderedDict with move_to_end(key) on access and popitem(last=False) on overflow to build an O(1) LRU cache.
Sort intervals by start, then merge by comparing each interval's start to the last merged interval's end to collapse overlaps in one pass.
Use a collections.deque holding indices as a monotonic queue, popping smaller trailing values, to get sliding-window maximum in O(n).
Implement topological sort with Kahn's algorithm: seed a deque with zero in-degree nodes, decrement neighbors' in-degree on pop, and flag a cycle if the output length is short of the node count.
Maintain a fixed-size min-heap with heapq.heappush then heapq.heappushpop once full to track the k largest elements in O(n log k).
Build a trie from nested dicts via node.setdefault(ch, {}) per character, marking word ends with a sentinel key like '$' to distinguish prefixes from full words.
